basis: Gas/oil steam & hot water dominate; biomass boilers dilute in some countries. Rehfeldt: food is the archetypal <100C subsector; Eurostat gas-heavy fuel mix.
Gas steam (sterilisation, evaporation). Same fuel pool as <100C.
Direct-fired drying/roasting, mostly gas; minor share of heat.
Dyeing/finishing hot water & steam, gas-dominated (Rehfeldt 2018 textiles profile).
Gas steam; very little biomass in this subsector.
Annealing lehr; gas or electric — fossil majority in EU average.
Melting furnaces predominantly natural gas (+ minority electric boost). High fossil at HIGH band.
Kiln: coal/petcoke + ~48%% EU alt-fuel thermal substitution (~half biogenic). NOT ~1.0 fossil — alt fuels lower it. Cembureau/ECRA.
Sinter/pickling; coke breeze + process gas (all fossil-derived).
Reheating furnaces; NG + coke-oven/BF gas, fossil.
High-temp reheating/sinter strand; fossil process gases.
USE Eurostat country×subsector value. Black liquor + bark (biomass) and fossil gas both serve steam; temperature does not separate them. Nordic ~0.05, continental ~0.5.
USE country value. Recovery-boiler & bark steam (biomass) vs fossil gas steam. This is where ~95%% of P&P heat sits.
USE country value; slightly higher fossil (lime kiln reburning uses gas/oil).
USE country value; lime kiln, more fossil.
Shaft/rotary kiln; gas/coal/coke, less alt-fuel than cement.
Ammonia/methanol reformers, fired heaters, natural gas.
Non-ferrous excl. aluminium; mix of electric and gas.
Cracking furnaces ~850C fired by fossil fuel-gas (CH4/H2 tail gas) + purchased gas. All fossil-derived.
Alumina calcination / anode bake; gas-fired (electrolysis itself is electric, excluded from fuel heat).
